pages.title: 28 Alloys
pages.text: TAIGA ships 28 alloy recipes that combine molten fluids in the smeltery. Each produces a higher-tier material from the one below:$(br2)$(item)Terrax$(), $(item)Triberium$() (two variants), $(item)Fractum$(), $(item)Violium$(), $(item)Proxii$(), $(item)Tritonite$(), $(item)Ignitz$(), $(item)Imperomite$(), $(item)Solarium$(), $(item)Adamant$(), $(item)Nihilite$()…

pages.title: Higher tiers
pages.text: $(item)Seismum$(), $(item)Astrium$(), $(item)Niob$(), $(item)Yrdeen$() (three variants), $(item)Iox$() (two variants), $(item)Lumix$(), $(item)Obsidiorite$(), $(item)Nucleum$() (three variants), $(item)Dyonite$() (two variants), $(item)Nitronite$().$(br2)Consult JEI for exact input ratios — they vary by alloy.

pages.title: Porting note
pages.text: The original 1.12 TAIGA used $(item)Ardite$() in several recipes. The TC3 port substitutes $(item)Amethyst Bronze$() wherever Ardite was called for — TC3 retired Ardite in 1.16+. Cobalt and obsidian references use standard $(o)tconstruct:molten_cobalt$() / $(o)tconstruct:molten_obsidian$() fluids.
